meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Learn how to build passive income and overcome project abandonment. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your first app.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects is scoping for 'Micro-SaaS' viability. By building small, revenue-generating tools powered by AI, you shift from endless learning to shipping. Use Antigravity to scaffold your MVP and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, turning your portfolio into a passive income stream that motivates completion.
content.intro:
You stare at the flickering cursor of a half-baked project, the graveyard of 'almost-done' repositories mounting in your GitHub. The excitement of the initial commit has long faded, replaced by the crushing weight of feature creep and technical debt. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a finish line.

Passive income isn't just about money—it's the ultimate accountability mechanism. When your code serves a user who pays, the project stops being a toy and becomes a business. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, we will transform your workflow from aimless exploration into a high-velocity production engine that ships real software.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a specific, small-scale pain point that takes less than 48 hours to solve. Focus on utility over complexity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product manager. Suggest 5 Micro-SaaS ideas that solve a specific pain point for students, require no database complexity, and can be monetized with a single Stripe link.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research 5 micro-saas ideas for students and output a lean product requirements document.'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Structure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular boilerplate that prevents 'spaghetti code' before you even write the first line.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean Next.js 14 architecture with Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons. Setup a structure that is strictly modular for rapid feature deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Bootstrap a Next.js 14 project with the requested file structure and install core dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement AI-Powered Logic
steps.description: Integrate Claude Code to handle the backend logic, ensuring your MVP functions perfectly without manual debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for an AI-powered tool that takes user input and returns structured JSON. Include error handling for API timeouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write a service layer for the AI logic and integrate it into the main dashboard component.'

steps.title: Automate the Payment Gate
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your project immediately. Nothing finishes a project faster than the prospect of a payment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a secure implementation guide for a Stripe checkout session in Next.js, focusing on a one-time payment flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the Stripe checkout route and create a mock success page.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your code to Vercel and share it with your first potential user. Completion is not perfection; it is deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a concise, professional announcement post for Twitter/X that highlights the specific problem my app solves.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review the project for production readiness and generate a deployment checklist.'

pro_tips: Always build the payment integration first; it forces you to define the value proposition immediately.
Use Claude Code to perform 'automated code reviews' on your own PRs to catch logic bugs before they become technical debt.
Limit your MVP to exactly three screens; anything more is a recipe for abandonment.
Treat your README.md as a product roadmap; if a feature isn't in the README, it doesn't exist.
Deploy to production on day one, even if the app is just a landing page, to build the mental habit of shipping.
content.next_steps: After completing your first MVP, focus on gathering user feedback for 7 days. Use that data to iterate on one single feature, then move to your next project to diversify your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the strategy to stop being a student who builds and start being a founder who ships. The only thing missing is your first commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
